patches.summary: Guard printAutoCards against short headers slices (len==0 early return, len>2 wrap before headers[2:]).
patches.reason: When prioritizeAllHeaders returned <2 entries the headers[2:] slice panicked with 'slice bounds out of range'. Surfaced by Greptile review of the initial print.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes; CLI no longer panics on items whose displayable fields collapse to 0/1 keys.

patches.summary: Replaced ftsRowID polynomial hash with FNV-1a 64-bit + NUL separator and added validIdentifierRE check to ResolveByName matchFields.
patches.reason: Polynomial hash had non-trivial collision probability for (scope, id) pairs; on collision DELETE WHERE rowid=? silently evicted an unrelated FTS row. ResolveByName interpolated matchFields into json_extract without the same identifier guard ListField uses, creating a future SQL-injection trap.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store passes; defence-in-depth aligns ResolveByName with ListField.

patches.summary: AddCandidate ON CONFLICT preserves killed=1 (sticky-kill); UpsertBatch renamed local 's' shadows to 'idStr'.
patches.reason: Shortlist promote always passes killed=0, so the unconditional ON CONFLICT killed=excluded.killed silently resurrected candidates the user had killed via `lists kill`. UpsertBatch's `s := fmt.Sprintf(...)` shadowed the *Store receiver, creating a confusing compile error trap for future edits.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store passes; shortlist promote no longer revives killed candidates.

patches.summary: Renamed budget --max-renewal to --max-annual-cost; updated help text, README, SKILL examples.
patches.reason: budget command treated --max-renewal as an amortised annual cap (total/years) but named the flag as if it capped the raw renewal price. gen suggest's --max-renewal correctly caps raw renewal and was left unchanged.
patches.validated_outcome: Help output shows the formula; smoke-tested both old budget call patterns rejected with unknown-flag and new flag accepted.

patches.summary: Documented at package level that Namecheap auth credentials travel in URL query params and rotation is the only mitigation.
patches.reason: Namecheap's API offers no header-based auth; callers/auditors should know up front that the only remediation for unexpected log exposure is dashboard key rotation.

patches.summary: readNamesFile reads all input into a single []byte before JSON vs plain-text branching.
patches.reason: Previous implementation opened the source twice; for path=='-' the second open returned the same os.Stdin fd after the first json.Decoder had buffered ~512 bytes internally, silently truncating plain-text input piped via `check --file -`.
patches.validated_outcome: Smoke-tested JSON array and plain-text (with # comments) stdin shapes via `check --file - --dry-run`; both round-trip cleanly.

patches.summary: Scan score/tld as sql.NullInt64/NullString; replaced MAX(fetched_at) join with ROW_NUMBER() window for per-fqdn dedup.
patches.reason: (a) Domains looked up via `rdap` (writes rdap_records only) were silently dropped from the timeline because the LEFT JOIN to `domains` produced NULL on score/tld and the plain-int scan errored. (b) Two writes within one second shared fetched_at (1s SQLite resolution), so MAX(fetched_at) matched both rows and the domain appeared twice.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes; rdap-only domains now appear once each in the timeline.

patches.summary: Capture http.NewRequestWithContext error in socials handler, record on the result row, skip the request instead of dereferencing nil req.
patches.reason: A handle with URL-unsafe characters (e.g. control bytes) made url.Parse fail, req nil, and req.Header.Set on the next line panicked.
patches.validated_outcome: Smoke-tested `socials` with control-char input — each platform returns the parse error as `note` with no crash.

patches.summary: AdaptiveLimiter advances lastRequest atomically inside the lock; sleeps until reserved instant outside the lock.
patches.reason: Previous read-then-sleep-then-write pattern let N parallel goroutines all read the same stale lastRequest, sleep identically, and fire simultaneously — defeating the per-session rate pacing under `check --parallel 8`.
patches.validated_outcome: Added TestAdaptiveLimiter_WaitPacesConcurrentCallers regression test: 5 concurrent Wait() calls at 10rps now take ~500ms (5 stacked slots) instead of ~100ms (all firing together).

patches.summary: tld-affinity inner SELECT...LIMIT 100 wrapped in IIFE with defer rows.Close() and rows.Err() zeroes partial counts.
patches.reason: Missing rows.Err() let a mid-scan cursor error produce a misleadingly low historical-availability estimate. Missing defer meant a hypothetical panic from rows.Scan would leak the SQL connection from the pool.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes; per-iteration close path is now deterministic on any exit.

patches.summary: Added Store.ListDueWatches with cadence-gated WHERE clause; `watch run` calls it by default; new --force flag falls back to ListWatches.
patches.reason: watch run iterated every domain on every cron tick regardless of cadence_hours, making the cadence configuration a no-op for users wiring `watch run` into cron.
patches.validated_outcome: Smoke-tested `watch run --dry-run` and `watch run --dry-run --force` — the dry_run payload reports the force flag end-to-end.

patches.summary: whoissrc.Lookup constructs a per-call whois.NewClient().SetTimeout(15s) so the goroutine terminates on TCP deadline rather than the library's 30s default.
patches.reason: likexian/whois has no ctx hook; the goroutine could be stranded on an open WHOIS socket for ~30s after the outer ctx already returned. Under check --parallel 8 against a slow server that was 8 sockets held simultaneously.
patches.validated_outcome: go vet/build clean; lookup deadline now matches outer time.After arm.

patches.summary: gen suggest --max-renewal now returns an error when openStore fails instead of silently dropping the filter.
patches.reason: Previous code wrapped the entire filter in `if err == nil`, so a failed store-open returned every candidate unfiltered — users got results that violated the budget ceiling they passed.
patches.validated_outcome: go vet/build clean; the filter is honoured or the command errors — never silently relaxes.

patches.summary: shortlist promote collects per-candidate AddCandidate failures and surfaces them under `errors` in the output map.
patches.reason: Errors were swallowed — operators saw `promoted < top` with no diagnostic on DB-full / ctx-cancel / writeMu-contention.
patches.validated_outcome: Output JSON now includes `errors` array when any candidate write fails; happy path unchanged.

patches.summary: drop-bid-window now falls through pendingDelete → redemption → expiration when computing the bid window.
patches.reason: Code extracted redemption from RDAP events but only computed windows for pendingDelete or expiration; domains in active redemption (30-day grace) returned an error even though redemption+35d was enough to estimate.
patches.validated_outcome: go vet/build clean; redemption-only domains now produce a window with basis 'redemption+35d (estimated; verify with later RDAP refresh)'.

patches.summary: SplitTLD doc comment updated to reflect actual first-dot (strings.Index) split behaviour.
patches.reason: Comment said 'last-dot split' but the code used strings.Index (first dot). Future readers debugging ccSLD edge cases could be misled.

patches.summary: Sort gen-suggest rows by score BEFORE the --available-only RDAP filter loop, so the count early-exit returns the top-N highest-scoring available domains.
patches.reason: gen.Generate emits candidates alphabetically; the availableOnly loop iterated in that order and broke at `count` matches, so users got the first N alphabetically-available domains rather than the top-N by brandability score (which --include-score=true makes the implicit goal).
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes; alphabetical-vs-score selection bias removed. The standalone `else if includeScore` sort block became unnecessary and was inlined into the unconditional pre-filter sort.
